what_it_does: An interim bash orchestrator that finishes wiring the Perplexity embedder into the transcript-search substrate after its weights finish downloading: it waits for a download-done marker, verifies four safetensor shards, starts the embed-pplx service with eviction, health-checks and smoke-tests the embedder, runs a full corpus index, performs a semantic search with a GPU-VRAM probe, runs a two-stage rerank across two Python environments, writes a JSON result file, and emits single-line MARKER events to stdout.
observations: Emits MARKER:<event> lines to stdout for an external Monitor.
Waits for ALL_SHARDS_DONE in /tmp/dl_shards.log and also detects if the download process dies.
Verifies four shard files under a specific HuggingFace snapshot path by size (>1GB).
Starts the embedder via a hardcoded `company up embed-pplx --wait --evict` command.
Polls http://127.0.0.1:8007/health up to 120 times with 10s sleeps.
Smoke-tests /v1/embeddings and expects exactly a 2560-dimensional int8 vector.
Runs a full re-index through a Python driver using the overlord venv.
Performs a semantic search using a grounded probe about GPU VRAM budgeting and model eviction.
Does two-stage reranking: substrate search dumps candidates in the overlord venv, then reranks in vllm-env on CPU with a configurable backend.
Writes a compact JSON result to /tmp/pplx_wire_result.json.
Uses `set -uo pipefail` but not `set -e`, so each step must check its own rc.
standouts: Structured MARKER event protocol for pipeline monitoring.
Cross-venv two-stage rerank (overlord search + vllm-env CPU reranker).
Semantic probe explicitly chosen to test retrieval of a real recurring topic rather than keyword matching.
Fail-loud design: any failure prints MARKER:FAIL and exits 1.
conventions: Follows a numbered step-by-step pipeline with section comments.
Uses hardcoded absolute paths and environment-specific Python interpreters.
Prefixes subcommand output with sed labels (up|, idx|, q|, rr|) for log readability.
Uses curl + python one-liners for quick JSON extraction.
concerns: Hardcoded paths and user home make the script environment-specific.
`set -uo pipefail` without `set -e` means uncaught failures in intermediate commands could go unnoticed if rc is not checked.
Relies on polling a log file and process presence, which could race if the download process exits right after writing the done marker.
Default reranker jina-v3 on CPU may be very slow; the script itself notes this.
Health check grep allows flexible whitespace but could match unintended strings.
notes: A throwaway but carefully instrumented glue script that turns a model download completion into a monitored, end-to-end embedding and reranking validation run.
questions: What process writes /tmp/dl_shards.log and how is ALL_SHARDS_DONE guaranteed to be flushed?
What are the exact interfaces of `company up embed-pplx` and the service on port 8007?
What is the contract of `company/ops/rerank.py` and how is the candidate JSON structured?
Which external Monitor consumes the MARKER events and how does it react to MARKER:FAIL?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual behavior: it is an interim throwaway that runs the gated tail of the transcript-search build once the pplx-embed weights are downloaded, including verification, service startup, indexing, search, and rerank.
intention_signals: The script is trying to provide a self-contained, monitorable validation pipeline that proves the new Perplexity embedder can be downloaded, loaded, health-checked, used to index the real corpus, and produce semantically relevant results improved by a separate reranker.
novelty: The explicit MARKER event protocol and the deliberate split between a lean, no-torch substrate search environment and a torch-equipped CPU rerank environment, with a real-corpus before/after comparison.
contribution: A concrete orchestration pattern that combines model readiness verification, service health validation, corpus indexing, semantic search, and cross-environment reranking into one observable, fail-loud script with machine-readable progress markers.
summary_for_embedding: Bash orchestrator that waits for Perplexity embedder weight download completion, verifies shards, starts and smoke-tests the embedder, indexes a transcript corpus, runs a semantic search, reranks results across two Python environments, and emits MARKER progress events.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an interim, monitorable end-to-end validation pipeline for integrating the pplx-embed model into the transcript-search substrate.

symbols.does: Prints a MARKER:<arg> line to stdout for the Monitor.
symbols.description: A small shell function used throughout the script to emit single-line MARKER events. It enables an external monitor to track pipeline progress, identify the current stage, and detect failure markers.
